projects.heading: Bidirectional sync between Google Sheets and MySQL
projects.title: DualSync
projects.description: Full Stack application for bidirectional sync between Google Sheets and MySQL
features: Utilized Redis through Upstash free tier and a free 1GB- storage MySQL database through Aiven free tier, Google sheet access through Google Sheets API
Since Google Sheets API doesnt provide native API for read/write operations, implemented a polling mechanism that checks for updates every 5 sec, and update the platform

A snapshot of the entire sheet info is taken once and stored in Redis database. In case of updation of sheet, the poller working in a 3s interval detects it,checks against the snapshot and eventually updates the snapshot and then the MySQL database.
In case of updatio of sheet through DB, if user executes INSERT/ UPDATE query, a Redis lock is set for 10s, the poller checks if there is a lock before updating the sheet
In case of 429 error(API limit exceeded), exponential backoff strategy implemented for retries
BullMQ queue implemented for task management, each task carries the job from either ends alongside the cell location

To prevent multiple updates to same cell, locking mechanism implemented using Redis locks, having 10s TTL, before updating the sheet, the poller checks if there is a lock for that cell, if there is it retries (5s->10s->20s) until the lock is released and then updates the sheet
In case of backend downtime-(MySQL/Sheet downtime), offline queue implemented, which stores updates in localStorage, and implements them once the connection is re-established

projects.heading: AI-PDF summarizer with citation provider
projects.title: AI Chat Citation Provider
projects.description: An AI chat interface where users can upload PDFs, and ask questions related to the content, the model provides answers supported by citations, alongside vizualizations of charts and tables.
features: Users can upload PDF documents and query documents using natural language, receiving real time streaming responses supported by citations from the pdfs themselves

Frontend is built using Next.js and Tailwind CSS, while backend is a FASTAPI server which handles the uploading of docs, RAG,LLM and vector db handling, as well as generating vizualizations(charts, tables etc.) using Vercel SDK

Using PymuPDF, pdfs are broken down into chunks and stored in ChromaDB vector db,with metadata like page no and source. During query, query is sent in queue as calls are made to SSE endpoint, and top 5 relevant chunks are retrived and sent for answer generation.
For generation of vizualizations, the LLM extracts relevant numerical data and feeds it to ui components through prop drilling.

projects.heading: All-in-one collection of user bookmarks/watch later
projects.title: Convergence
projects.description: [IN PROGRESS] A platform where users can access bookmarks and to read/watch later items from different platforms- Reddit, X,Chrome bookmarks, Youtube etc. in one place, available to users through searching in natural language

Extracted user-saved Reddit bookmarks by polling the Reddit bookmark RSS feed
[IN PROGRESS]- Scraping X bookmarks and Instagram reel bookmarked using Chrome bookmarks- and working on a cron-job to update for new saved items
